skill.name: supabase-postgres-best-practices
skill.description: Supabase guidance for writing, reviewing, and optimizing Postgres queries, schemas, indexes, RLS policies, and connection settings.
skill.summary: Apply Supabase Postgres performance and security best practices to database design and review.
skill.author: supabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but targeted review found documentation examples rather than executable malware or install-time behavior. Command, network, crypto, and script alerts are false positives from Markdown SQL examples, contributor instructions, and documentation links. No prompt injection text, data exfiltration logic, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged shell-like text in README and skill files, but the content is contributor documentation and file path examples. No runtime script, hook, or installer executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown examples and rule references, not executable source code. The repository files reviewed are documentation-oriented Markdown and JSON.

low_findings.title: SQL COPY Syntax Misclassified as Script Risk
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The analyzer treated SQL COPY options using WITH as a script pattern. The lines are examples for bulk loading data in Postgres documentation, not JavaScript with statements or executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a SQL code block showing COPY syntax. There is no dynamic code execution or scoped scripting behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Hardcoded URLs point to Supabase and PostgreSQL documentation references. I found no evidence that the skill performs network requests or sends data to external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static citations in Markdown and metadata. No fetch, curl, webhook, or client library call was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Cryptography Alerts Are Database Terminology
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak-cryptography alerts appear to be triggered by database terms such as UUID and hash in best-practice examples. These examples discuss identifier locality and advisory lock keys, not cryptographic security controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are metadata tags or SQL guidance. They do not implement hashing, encryption, password storage, or signature verification.

content.value_statement: Postgres performance problems often come from missing indexes, poor RLS policies, and inefficient access patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Supabase guidance for safer database reviews.
actual_capabilities: Reviews SQL queries for missing indexes, inefficient joins, and pagination issues.
Suggests B-tree, GIN, BRIN, composite, covering, and partial index patterns.
Guides Supabase RLS policy design and performance optimization.
Explains connection pooling, connection limits, and prepared statement tradeoffs.
Recommends schema choices for data types, primary keys, partitions, and foreign keys.
Provides monitoring guidance using EXPLAIN ANALYZE, pg_stat_statements, VACUUM, and ANALYZE.
limitations: Does not connect to a live database or inspect real query plans automatically.
Does not replace load testing, production monitoring, or DBA review for high-risk migrations.
Examples must be adapted to the application schema, workload, and Supabase project limits.
Some recommendations may require privileges that managed environments restrict.

use_cases.title: Review a Slow Query
use_cases.description: Identify likely missing indexes, poor filters, and query rewrites before changing application code.

use_cases.title: Plan a Supabase Schema
use_cases.description: Choose data types, primary keys, foreign key indexes, and RLS policies for a new product feature.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Database Migration Review
use_cases.description: Check connection, locking, partitioning, and monitoring risks before shipping a migration.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one SQL query
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Postgres query using Supabase best practices. Identify missing indexes, likely scan risks, and one safer rewrite if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Design Indexes
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these tables and common WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY, and SELECT patterns, recommend Postgres indexes. Explain column order and any partial or covering indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit RLS Policies
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Supabase RLS policies for correctness and performance. Look for per-row function calls, missing indexes, and unsafe privilege assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Tuning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production readiness review for this Postgres workload. Cover pooling, timeouts, lock contention, pagination, batching, monitoring, and migration risks.

output_examples.input: A query filters orders by status and created_at but only has separate single-column indexes.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a composite index with equality columns before range columns, then explains when separate indexes may still help.

output_examples.input: A Supabase policy calls auth.uid() directly in a policy used on a large table.
output_examples.output: The skill flags the per-row function cost, suggests wrapping the function in a select expression, and reminds the user to index the policy column.

output_examples.input: An API endpoint uses OFFSET pagination on a growing events table.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends cursor-based pagination with a stable ordering column and an index that matches the cursor condition.

best_practices: Provide table definitions, query text, filters, joins, and expected row counts when asking for review.
Ask for EXPLAIN ANALYZE interpretation when you have an actual plan from the target database.
Validate every suggested index or migration against production-like data before release.
anti_patterns: Do not add indexes blindly without matching them to real WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY, or RLS patterns.
Do not run broad ALTER SYSTEM or connection changes without checking Supabase project limits.
Do not treat example SQL as ready for production without adapting names, constraints, and privileges.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users must run any SQL themselves after review.

faq.question: Can it optimize Supabase Row Level Security policies?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for RLS correctness and performance, including indexes and function-call patterns.

faq.question: Does it require access to a Supabase project?
faq.answer: No. It can review SQL and schemas from text. Live database access is not part of the skill.

faq.question: Can it choose indexes for JSONB and full-text search?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers GIN indexes for JSONB and tsvector-based full-text search patterns.

faq.question: Is it useful outside Supabase?
faq.answer: Yes. Most guidance applies to Postgres generally, with some Supabase-specific notes for pooling and RLS.

faq.question: Will it guarantee faster queries?
faq.answer: No. It gives likely improvements, but results depend on data size, distribution, query plans, and workload.
